notes: Built app served locally via next start on port 3001
Live requests were sent through the app's real generation path
Summary judgments were then made manually against the repo's fidelity rubric and explicit case truths/forbidden additions

validation.lintFailure: ESLint 10 flat config missing; eslint.config.* not present

overall.strongestVulnerability: Conflict adjudication instead of conflict preservation in assessment-level prose
secondaryVulnerabilities: Transcript-vs-clinician contradiction handling for recent self-harm
Objective data and collateral can overpower denial too cleanly
Narrow-plan detection still produces section-level awkwardness

cases.title: Mother reports active SI while patient denies current SI
strengths: Preserved patient denial of current SI/plan/intent
Preserved mother-reported concerning text from last night
Kept disagreement explicit
Did not invent attempt, hold, or disposition
issues: Chief-concern framing sounds slightly more resolved than the source conflict warrants
Could use a more specific current-denial-vs-recent-collateral-risk warning
warningsFired: Risk language may need more detail
Plan details may be narrower than they look
warningsShouldHaveFired: Current denial vs recent collateral-reported SI warning

cases.title: Hallucinations denied but behavior suggests internal preoccupation
strengths: Preserved denial of AH/VH
Preserved behavioral observations and nursing concern
Preserved haloperidol administration
Did not invent med changes or discharge planning
issues: Assessment turns behavior into a more settled internal-preoccupation conclusion than the source fully earns
Needs a stronger psychosis-denial vs observed-behavior guardrail
warningsFired: Objective data may conflict with the narrative
Plan details may be narrower than they look
warningsShouldHaveFired: Psychosis denial versus observed internal preoccupation warning

cases.title: Sertraline dose conflict across clinician note, patient report, and chart
strengths: Strong preservation of 50 mg vs 100 mg conflict
Preserved anxiety as about the same
Preserved unreviewed pharmacy history
Did not invent today's medication decision
issues: Assessment still reads slightly more settled than the underlying adherence/chart conflict
Could use a more direct unresolved-active-dose warning
warningsShouldHaveFired: Medication conflict / active dose unresolved warning

cases.title: Substance use denied despite positive screen and collateral concern
strengths: Preserved patient denial
Preserved collateral report and positive UDS
Preserved irritable mood and no SI/HI
Did not invent detox, hospitalization, or detailed intoxication claims
issues: Assessment adjudicates the conflict too strongly by concluding recent cocaine use from mixed evidence
Timing and pattern ambiguity is not preserved sharply enough
warningsFired: Collateral details may be mixed into other sections
Plan details may be narrower than they look
warningsShouldHaveFired: Patient denial vs positive screen/collateral conflict warning
Avoid turning conflicting evidence into a settled use pattern cue

cases.title: No self-harm in clinician note but transcript discloses recent cutting
strengths: Preserved recent cutting disclosure
Preserved denial of suicidal intent
Did not label the act a suicide attempt
Did not invent wound severity or treatment
issues: Symptom-review wording becomes internally contradictory by saying no self-harm was reported while also documenting cutting disclosure
Assessment drifts toward a stronger NSSI-style label than the exact source wording
warningsFired: Risk language may need more detail
Plan details may be narrower than they look
warningsShouldHaveFired: Transcript overrides cleaner clinician summary warning
Recent self-harm disclosure conflicts with no-self-harm summary warning
Do not up-label cutting disclosure into broader NSSI terminology unless explicit

cases.title: Passive homicidal fantasy versus active violent intent
strengths: Preserved violent-thought content
Preserved denial of intent, plan, weapon access, and steps toward harm
Preserved no recent assaultive behavior
Preserved brother-based coping strategy
issues: Assessment slightly smooths vivid source language into cleaner generic phrasing
Plan section awkwardly includes supported coping content and then says plan details were not documented
warningsFired: Plan details may be narrower than they look
warningsShouldHaveFired: Violent fantasy vs active intent warning

recommendedNextMove: Tighten assessment-layer prompt behavior so unresolved source conflicts stay unresolved in the note. Specifically add explicit conflict-preservation rules and warning cues for cases 24, 23, and 21, then rerun those three first.
